template-method.tooltip.obj-csv: Runtime CSVMiner instance after mining pipeline
template-method.tooltip.obj-json: Runtime JSONMiner instance — same template, different format
template-method.tooltip.obj-xml: Runtime XMLMiner instance — same template, different format
template-method.step.mining.0: Call mine() template method on CSVMiner
template-method.step.mining.4: Step 4: analyze() — common logic in base class
template-method.step.mining.5: Step 5: sendReport() — common logic in base class
template-method.step.mining.7: Same template works for JSON/XML miners
template-method.stepLabel.mining: Data mining pipeline via Template Method

template-method.depRules.name: DataMiner (Abstract Class)
template-method.depRules.role: Defines mine() template method with algorithm skeleton

template-method.depRules.name: CSVMiner
template-method.depRules.role: Overrides steps for CSV file processing

template-method.depRules.name: JSONMiner
template-method.depRules.role: Overrides steps for JSON file processing

template-method.depRules.name: XMLMiner
template-method.depRules.role: Overrides steps for XML file processing

visitor.name: Visitor
visitor.modes.export.label: Shape Export
visitor.modes.export.desc: An XMLExportVisitor and JSONExportVisitor traverse a collection of shapes (Circle, Rectangle) via double dispatch. Each shape's accept(visitor) method calls the appropriate visit method, allowing new export formats to be added without modifying shape classes.
visitor.details.export.principles: Double dispatch: accept() calls the visitor's type-specific method, enabling polymorphism on both sides
Open/Closed Principle: new operations (visitors) can be added without modifying element classes
Single Responsibility: each visitor encapsulates one operation across all element types
Separation of concerns: algorithms are separated from the objects they operate on
Accumulation: visitors can gather state as they traverse the object structure

visitor.details.export.concepts.definition: The client creates a visitor, holds a collection of elements (shapes), and iterates them calling accept(visitor) on each — driving the double dispatch process.
An interface declaring visit methods for each element type (visitCircle, visitRectangle). Each concrete visitor implements a specific operation.
Shape interface with accept(visitor) method. Each concrete element calls the appropriate visit method on the visitor.
Two polymorphic calls: first on the element (accept), then on the visitor (visitX). This selects the correct method based on both types.

visitor.details.export.tradeoffs.pros: Easy to add new operations — just create a new visitor class
Related operations are grouped in one visitor, not scattered across element classes
Visitors can accumulate state while traversing the object structure
Follows Single Responsibility — each visitor handles one concern
visitor.details.export.tradeoffs.cons: Adding new element types requires updating all visitors
Visitors may break encapsulation if they need access to element internals
Double dispatch can be confusing and hard to debug
Not suitable when element classes change frequently
visitor.details.export.tradeoffs.whenToU: Use when you need to perform many unrelated operations on objects in a structure and want to avoid polluting their classes, when the object structure rarely changes but new operations are added frequently, or when you want to accumulate state across a complex object structure.
visitor.tooltip.client: Client — creates visitors, iterates shapes, and calls accept(visitor) on each
visitor.tooltip.visitor: Visitor interface — declares visitCircle() and visitRectangle() methods
visitor.tooltip.shape: Shape interface — declares accept(visitor) for double dispatch
visitor.tooltip.xml: XMLExportVisitor exports shapes to XML format
visitor.tooltip.json: JSONExportVisitor exports shapes to JSON format
visitor.tooltip.circle: Circle shape with radius, implements accept(visitor)
visitor.tooltip.rect: Rectangle shape with width and height, implements accept(visitor)
visitor.tooltip.obj-json: Runtime JSONExportVisitor instance — same traversal, different output
visitor.step.export.9: All shapes exported — no shape code changed

visitor.depRules.name: Client
visitor.depRules.role: Creates visitors, iterates shapes, and calls accept(visitor) on each

visitor.depRules.name: Visitor (interface)
visitor.depRules.role: Declares visit methods for each shape type

visitor.depRules.name: Rectangle
visitor.depRules.role: Concrete shape with width and height
